channel.title: MyPKM
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Tutorial 1 - First Simulation in Webots
item.description:
Goto "File" => "New" => "Project Directory"
Choose your project directory path

Tick "Add a rectangle arena", name it "My First World"
Click "Finish"

Double-click on the RectangleArena node in the scene tree. This should open the node and display its fields.

Select the floorTileSize field and set its value to 0.25 0.25 instead of 0.5 0.5. You should see the effect immediately in the 3D view.
Select the wallHeight field and change its value to 0.05 instead of 0.1. The wall of the arena should now be lower.


Double-click on the RectangleArena in the scene tree to close it and select it. Click on the Add button  at the top of the scene tree. In the open dialog box, choose PROTO nodes (Webots Projects) / objects / factory / containers / WoodenBox (Solid). A big box should appear in the middle of the arena. Double-click on it in the scene tree to open its fields.

Change its size to 0.1 0.1 0.1 instead of 0.6 0.6 0.6.
Change its translation to 0 0 0.05 instead of 0 0 0.3. Alternatively, you may use the blue arrow that appears in the 3D view to adjust its translation.z field.
Now shift-click and drag the box in the 3D view and move it in some corner of the arena.
Select the box and press ctrl-C, ctrl-V (Windows, Linux) or ⌘ command-C, ⌘ command-V (macOS) to copy and paste it. Shift-click and drag the new box to move it at some different location. Create a third box this way.
Move the boxes, so that no box is at the center of the arena. You may also use the blue rotation arrows to rotate the boxes along the vertical axis. This can be done also by shift-click and drag with the right mouse button. Alternatively, you can change the angle of the rotation field of the WoodenBox nodes in the scene tree.
Once you are satisfied with the result, save the world using the save button.


The e-puck is a small robot having differential wheels, 10 LEDs, and several sensors including 8 DistanceSensors and a Camera.
Now, we are going to add an e-puck model to the world. Make sure that the simulation is paused and that the virtual time elapsed is 0. If this is not the case, reset the simulation with the Reset button .
Select the last node WoodenBox of the scene tree view. Click on the Add button  at the top of the scene tree view. In the dialog box, choose PROTO nodes (Webots Projects) / robots / gctronic / e-puck / E-puck (Robot). An e-puck robot should appear in the middle of the arena. Move and rotate this robot, the same way you did it with the boxes. Save the simulation and press the Run real-time button .


Create a new C (or any other language) controller called epuck_go_forward (for Python call it EPuckGoForward instead) using the File / New / New Robot Controller... menu item. This will create a new epuck_go_forward (or EPuckGoForward) directory in my_first_simulation/controllers. Select the option offering you to open the source file in the text editor.

![[Pasted image 20250422133651.png]

Try the code below"
from controller import Robot TIME_STEP = 64 # Create the Robot instance robot = Robot() # Get the motor devices left_motor = robot.getDevice("left wheel motor") right_motor = robot.getDevice("right wheel motor") # Set the target position of the motors left_motor.setPosition(10.0) right_motor.setPosition(10.0) # Run the simulation until manually stopped while robot.step(TIME_STEP) != -1: pass

Load the controller

Save the code.


from controller import Robot, Motor TIME_STEP = 64 MAX_SPEED = 6.28 # create the Robot instance robot = Robot() # get motor devices left_motor = robot.getDevice("left wheel motor") right_motor = robot.getDevice("right wheel motor") # set motor positions to infinity for velocity control left_motor.setPosition(float('inf')) right_motor.setPosition(float('inf')) # set initial velocity to 10% of MAX_SPEED left_motor.setVelocity(0.1 * MAX_SPEED) right_motor.setVelocity(0.1 * MAX_SPEED) # main loop while robot.step(TIME_STEP) != -1: pass # do nothing, just keep motors running # cleanup is automatic in Python, no need for explicit call


To write a C program for a 3-puck mobile robot to perform an L-path movement with time-based control
Reference : Michel, O. (2004). Webots: Professional mobile robot simulation. International Journal of Advanced Robotic Systems, 1(1), 39–42. https://doi.org/10.5772/5692
yberbotics Ltd. (n.d.). Webots [Computer software]. Retrieved from https://cyberbotics.com


Go to "file" => "Open Sample World" => type "robot_programming" and find it

item.title: Tutorial 2 - Using Wheel Encoder for Square Path Movement (sensor-based control)
item.description:
Program that controls a Pioneer robot to follow a 2m by 2m square path.
 Pioneer 3-DX:
The diameter of the wheels is 195mm.
The distance between the two wheels is 330mm.
Go to "file" => "Open Sample World" => type "square_path" and find it


The sample code use time based control. It has a limitation, and inconsistent performance when the battery of robot drops.

Enhance the code by using the wheel encoder.



The sample code of moving the robot:
from controller import Robot TIME_STEP = 64 MAX_VEL = 5.24 # create the Robot instance. robot = Robot() # get the motor devices left_motor = robot.getDevice('left wheel') right_motor = robot.getDevice('right wheel') left_motor.setPosition(float("inf")) right_motor.setPosition(float("inf")) while robot.step(TIME_STEP) != -1: left_motor.setVelocity(MAX_VEL) right_motor.setVelocity(MAX_VEL)

The sample code of getting encoder values:
from controller import Robot import time TIME_STEP = 64 SPEED = 3.0 robot = Robot() left_motor = robot.getDevice("left wheel") right_motor = robot.getDevice("right wheel") left_motor.setPosition(float("inf")) right_motor.setPosition(float("inf")) left_sensor = robot.getDevice("left wheel sensor") right_sensor = robot.getDevice("right wheel sensor") left_sensor.enable(TIME_STEP) right_sensor.enable(TIME_STEP) # Stop left_motor.setVelocity(0) right_motor.setVelocity(0) # wait for the robot the boot up for _ in range(10):     robot.step(TIME_STEP) # Get the encoder sensor values initial_left = left_sensor.getValue() initial_right = right_sensor.getValue() for side in range(4):     initial_left = left_sensor.getValue()     initial_right = right_sensor.getValue()     print(f"initial_left: {initial_left:.2f}")     for _ in range(10):         robot.step(TIME_STEP)
Reference : Michel, O. (2004). Webots: Professional mobile robot simulation. International Journal of Advanced Robotic Systems, 1(1), 39–42. https://doi.org/10.5772/5692
yberbotics Ltd. (n.d.). Webots [Computer software]. Retrieved from https://cyberbotics.com
item.link: Webots_Sensors\Tutorial 2 - Using Wheel Encoder for Square Path Movement (sensor-based control).html
item.guid: Webots_Sensors/Tutorial 2 - Using Wheel Encoder for Square Path Movement (sensor-based control).md

item.title: Tutorial 3 - Line following
item.description:
Download the zip file here
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1b2_Agc4ajRoYhzT54AAUX5EhKAJdQq2Q/view?usp=sharing

To initialize the ground sensors:
gs = [] gsNames = ['gs0', 'gs1', 'gs2'] for i in range(3): gs.append(robot.getDevice(gsNames[i])) gs[i].enable(timestep)
To read the ground sensors inside the main loop:
gsValues = [] for i in range(3): gsValues.append(gs[i].getValue())
To read sensor values inside the main loop:
line_right = gsValues[0] line_center = gsValues[1] line_left = gsValues[2]
Complete the line following robot coding using using state-machine code





"right click" on "RectangleArena"
"Add new"
Find "bottle"
Click on the arrow, to move the object

Reference : Lima, J., & Martins, F. N. (n.d.). Lab 2 – Line-follower with State Machine. Robotics-Simulation-Labs. Retrieved August 12, 2025, from Felipe N. Martins GitHub Pages: https://felipenmartins.github.io/Robotics-Simulation-Labs/Lab2/

item.title: Motors for Thymio II robot
item.description:
Go to "file" => "Open Sample World" => type "obstacle_avoidance" and find it

This benchmark aims to create a robust and efficient algorithm for obstacle avoidance for the Thymio II robot, using the Python programming language.
The goal is for the robot to cross the room and reach the opposite wall as fast as possible, while avoiding all collisions with obstacles.
To encourage robust avoidance behaviour, the obstacles are randomly positioned each run.
The benchmark metric t is the time taken for the robot to cross the room. Minimizing this time is the goal for this scenario.
The timer stops after either the robot is within 40 cm of the back wall or more than 1 minute 20 seconds has elapsed.
Any collision with obstacles in the room is considered an immediate failure to complete the task, and a time of 1 minute 20 seconds (the maximum time) is recorded.

leftMotor = robot.getDevice("motor.left") rightMotor = robot.getDevice("motor.right")

Sample Code
from controller import Robot # Get reference to the robot. robot = Robot() # Get simulation step length. timeStep = int(robot.getBasicTimeStep()) # Get frontal distance sensors. outerLeftSensor = robot.getDevice("prox.horizontal.0") centralLeftSensor = robot.getDevice("prox.horizontal.1") centralSensor = robot.getDevice("prox.horizontal.2") centralRightSensor = robot.getDevice("prox.horizontal.3") outerRightSensor = robot.getDevice("prox.horizontal.4") # Enable distance sensors. outerLeftSensor.enable(timeStep) centralLeftSensor.enable(timeStep) centralSensor.enable(timeStep) centralRightSensor.enable(timeStep) outerRightSensor.enable(timeStep) while robot.step(timeStep) != -1: # Read values from four distance sensors and calibrate. outerLeftSensorValue = outerLeftSensor.getValue() centralLeftSensorValue = centralLeftSensor.getValue() centralSensorValue = centralSensor.getValue() centralRightSensorValue = centralRightSensor.getValue() outerRightSensorValue = outerRightSensor.getValue()

Complete Guide for HMC5883L Magnetometer /Digital Compass | Senso...

Magnetic sensors - GebraBit

Sample Code:
from controller import Robot, Compass # Create robot instance robot = Robot() timeStep = int(robot.getBasicTimeStep()) # Initialize Compass compass = robot.getDevice("compass") compass.enable(timeStep) def get_heading_angle(compass_values): rad = math.atan2(compass_values[0], compass_values[1]) if rad < 0: rad += 2 * math.pi return rad while robot.step(timeStep) != -1: compass_values = compass.getValues() current_heading = get_heading_angle(compass_values)
Reference : Michel, O. (2004). Webots: Professional mobile robot simulation. International Journal of Advanced Robotic Systems, 1(1), 39–42. https://doi.org/10.5772/5692
yberbotics Ltd. (n.d.). Webots [Computer software]. Retrieved from https://cyberbotics.com

item.title: Tutorial 5 - Odometry
item.description:
The encoder (with pulse input) odometry formula:

Modify to encoder (with radian input)



Report the robot's coordinate using the formula above when the robot is moving the square path movement.

item.title: Tutorial 6 - Wall Following
item.description:
Go to "file" => "Open Sample World" => type "wall_following" and find it

Programe a Pioneer robot to follow a wall.
The robot has exactly 1 minute to go as far as possible, while trying to stay at a 50cm distance from the wall.
The benchmark will first compute an "ideal" path. The first segment of this path is a direct line going from the starting position of the robot to the closest point which is 50cm away from the wall. The path then continues parallel to the wall, at a distance of exactly 50cm.
The performance of your robot will be computed based on the average distance to this "ideal" path. Note that the robot is always facing the wall in its starting position, so the first part will always be to go forward until the wall is 50cm away.


Additionally, if the robot tries to take a shortcut, it will get a penalty for each skipped segment. At any given time, the segment of the path that is closest to the robot is considered as the current segment, and any segment prior to the current segment that has not been visited will be considered as skipped.
The sensors of the Pioneer 3-DX have the following configuration:



There is an angle of 20° between adjacent sensors, except for the side sensors (so0, so7, so8 and so15), which have a 40° angle. The maximum range of the sensors is 5m.
Because the sensors of the Pioneer 3-DX are sonars, they can only detect an obstacle within their angle of incidence, which is 22.5° in this case. In other words, sonar rays which lie outside the reflexion cone of aperture 45° are not picked up by the sonar which causes the obstacle to be "invisible" to this particular sensor.

Sample code:
"""Sample Webots controller for the wall following benchmark.""" from controller import Robot def getDistance(sensor): """ Return the distance of an obstacle for a sensor. The value returned by the getValue() method of the distance sensors corresponds to a physical value (here we have a sonar, so it is the strength of the sonar ray). This function makes a conversion to a distance value in meters. """ return ((1000 - sensor.getValue()) / 1000) * 5 # Maximum speed for the velocity value of the wheels. # Don't change this value. MAX_SPEED = 5.24 # Get pointer to the robot. robot = Robot() # Get the time step of the current world. timestep = int(robot.getBasicTimeStep()) # Get pointer to the robot wheels motors. leftWheel = robot.getDevice('left wheel') rightWheel = robot.getDevice('right wheel') # We will use the velocity parameter of the wheels, so we need to # set the target position to infinity. leftWheel.setPosition(float('inf')) rightWheel.setPosition(float('inf')) # Get and enable the distance sensors. frontSensor = robot.getDevice("so3") frontSensor.enable(timestep) sideSensor = robot.getDevice("so0") sideSensor.enable(timestep) # Move forward until we are 50 cm away from the wall. leftWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) rightWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) while robot.step(timestep) != -1: if getDistance(frontSensor) < 0.5: break # Rotate clockwise until the wall is to our left. leftWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) rightWheel.setVelocity(-MAX_SPEED) while robot.step(timestep) != -1: # Rotate until there is a wall to our left, and nothing in front of us. if getDistance(sideSensor) < 1: break # Main loop. while robot.step(timestep) != -1: # Too close to the wall, we need to turn right. if getDistance(sideSensor) < 0.4: leftWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) rightWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED * 0.9) # Too far from the wall, we need to turn left. elif getDistance(sideSensor) > 0.6: leftWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED * 0.9) rightWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) # We are in the right direction. else: leftWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) rightWheel.setVelocity(MAX_SPEED) # Stop the robot when we are done. leftWheel.setVelocity(0) rightWheel.setVelocity(0)
Reference : Michel, O. (2004). Webots: Professional mobile robot simulation. International Journal of Advanced Robotic Systems, 1(1), 39–42. https://doi.org/10.5772/5692
yberbotics Ltd. (n.d.). Webots [Computer software]. Retrieved from https://cyberbotics.com

item.title: Tutorial 8 - Pit Escape
item.description:
Go to "file" => "Open Sample World" => type "pit escape" and find it

Code BB-8 robot to escape from a pit.
Help the robot to get out of the pit as fast as possible.
The slope is too steep for the robot to simply go forward. Instead, it should go back and forth to accumulate momentum until it has enough to climb out of the pit.
The basic controller simply goes back and forth at regular time intervals. You should try to find a better way to decide when to change direction to optimize the accumulated momentum. For example you can use the gyro sensor of the robot to measure the current velocity of the robot.
The gyro can be initialized using the following piece of code:

gyro = robot.getGyro("body gyro") gyro.enable(timestep)
You can then use
gyro.getValues()
to read the current value of the sensor.

The gyroscope measures angular velocity around the x, y, z axes of the robot.
-

item.title: Webots Installation
item.description:
check python installation and version
python --version

Install it if python is not install yet
Press Win + R, type cmd, and press Enter.
run this command
curl -o python-installer.exe https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.12.0/python-3.12.0-amd64.exe
python-installer.exe InstallAllUsers=1 PrependPath=1

check the installation
python --version

Download the installer from https://cyberbotics.com/
Reference:
Michel, O. (2004). Webots: Professional mobile robot simulation. International Journal of Advanced Robotic Systems, 1(1), 39–42. https://doi.org/10.5772/5692

item.title: Convert to Label Studio from RobotFlow
item.description: import json import os # Load your Roboflow COCO JSON file with open("_annotations.coco.json") as f: coco = json.load(f) # Build category lookup categories = {cat["id"]: cat["name"] for cat in coco["categories"]} # Build image lookup images = {img["id"]: img for img in coco["images"]} # Optional: define your image path prefix for Label Studio to locate the images IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX = "/data/local-files/?d=train/" # Collect annotations per image annotations_by_image = {} for ann in coco.get("annotations", []): img_id = ann["image_id"] bbox = ann["bbox"] # [x, y, width, height] category_id = ann["category_id"] image = images[img_id] width = image["width"] height = image["height"] # Normalize coordinates for Label Studio x = bbox[0] / width * 100 y = bbox[1] / height * 100 w = bbox[2] / width * 100 h = bbox[3] / height * 100 result = { "from_name": "label", "to_name": "image", "type": "rectanglelabels", "value": { "x": x, "y": y, "width": w, "height": h, "rectanglelabels": [categories[category_id]], }, } if img_id not in annotations_by_image: annotations_by_image[img_id] = [] annotations_by_image[img_id].append(result) # Convert to Label Studio format label_studio_data = [] for img_id, image in images.items(): entry = { "data": {"image": IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX + image["file_name"]}, "annotations": [{"result": annotations_by_image.get(img_id, [])}], } label_studio_data.append(entry) # Save to file with open("label_studio_format.json", "w") as f: json.dump(label_studio_data, f, indent=2) print("Conversion complete. Output saved to label_studio_format.json.") print("Categories:") for cat_id, cat_name in categories.items(): print(f"{cat_id}: {cat_name}")
item.link: YOLO_LabelStudio\Convert_RobotFlow_To_LabelStudio and merger\Convert to Label Studio from RobotFlow.html
item.guid: YOLO_LabelStudio/Convert_RobotFlow_To_LabelStudio and merger/Convert to Label Studio from RobotFlow.md

item.title: Convert YOLO to Json Label Studio
item.description: import os import json from PIL import Image import uuid # === Configuration === yolo_path = "C:\\Users\\user\\Desktop\\{Yolo folder}" # Change this to your folder path images_dir = os.path.join(yolo_path, "images") labels_dir = os.path.join(yolo_path, "labels") classes_file = os.path.join(yolo_path, "classes.txt") IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX = "/data/local-files/?d=train/" # === Load class names === with open(classes_file, "r") as f: classes = [line.strip() for line in f.readlines()] # === Prepare Label Studio tasks === label_studio_tasks = [] for label_file in os.listdir(labels_dir): if not label_file.endswith(".txt"): continue image_name = label_file.replace(".txt", ".jpg") # or use .png if needed image_path = os.path.join(images_dir, image_name) if not os.path.exists(image_path): print(f"Image not found: {image_name}, skipping.") continue width, height = Image.open(image_path).size annotations = [] with open(os.path.join(labels_dir, label_file), "r") as f: for line in f: parts = line.strip().split() if len(parts) < 5: print(f"Skipping malformed line in {label_file}: {line.strip()}") continue class_id, x_center, y_center, w, h = map(float, parts[:5]) annotations.append( { "id": None, "value": { "x": (x_center - w / 2) * 100, "y": (y_center - h / 2) * 100, "width": w * 100, "height": h * 100, "rotation": 0, "rectanglelabels": [classes[int(class_id)]], }, "from_name": "label", "to_name": "image", "type": "rectanglelabels", } ) task = { "data": {"image": f"/data/local-files/?d=train/images/{image_name}"}, "annotations": [{"result": annotations}], } label_studio_tasks.append(task) # === Save output JSON === output_path = os.path.join(yolo_path, "label_studio_import.json") with open(output_path, "w") as f: json.dump(label_studio_tasks, f, indent=2) print(f"\n✅ Successfully created: {output_path}")
item.link: YOLO_LabelStudio\Convert_RobotFlow_To_LabelStudio and merger\Convert YOLO to Json Label Studio.html
item.guid: YOLO_LabelStudio/Convert_RobotFlow_To_LabelStudio and merger/Convert YOLO to Json Label Studio.md

item.title: Merger two Datasets
item.description: import json # Load both datasets with open("label_studio_format.json", "r") as f1: data1 = json.load(f1) with open("label_studio_format_2.json", "r") as f2: data2 = json.load(f2) # Merge the lists merged_data = data1 + data2 # Extract all unique labels all_labels = set() for task in merged_data: for annotation in task.get("annotations", []): for result in annotation.get("result", []): labels = result.get("value", {}).get("rectanglelabels", []) all_labels.update(labels) # Save merged data with open("merged_label_studio.json", "w") as f: json.dump(merged_data, f, indent=2) # Save the distinct categories sorted_labels = sorted(all_labels) with open("categories.json", "w") as f: json.dump(sorted_labels, f, indent=2) # Create Label Studio Interface XML color_palette = [ "orange", "blue", "red", "green", "purple", "pink", "gray", "brown", "cyan", "yellow", ] xml_lines = [ "<View>", ' <Image name="image" value="$image" zoom="true"/>', ' <RectangleLabels name="label" toName="image">', ] for i, label in enumerate(sorted_labels): color = color_palette[i % len(color_palette)] xml_lines.append(f' <Label value="{label}" background="{color}"/>') xml_lines += [" </RectangleLabels>", "</View>"] # Save the XML interface_xml = "\n".join(xml_lines) with open("label_studio_interface.xml", "w") as f: f.write(interface_xml) print("✅ Merged file saved as 'merged_label_studio.json'") print("✅ Categories saved as 'categories.json'") print("✅ Label Studio interface XML saved as 'label_studio_interface.xml'")

item.title: 1 Installing Label Studio
item.description:
Search "cmd" from windows, run as administrator
.
Check installed python
python3 --version

download the python installer, if there is no python installed.
https://www.python.org/downloads/

Navigate to your Documents folder
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents"

Create a folder
mkdir yolo_projects && cd yolo_projects

Create the virtual environment:
python3 -m venv venv39

Activate the environment
cd venv39 && Scripts\activate


Install Label-Studio
pip install label-studio

item.title: 2 Image Annotation
item.description:
Search "cmd" from windows, run as administrator
.
Navigate to your Documents folder
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents" && cd yolo_projects

create a folder, "dataset"
mkdir dataset


Open this website, https://universe.roboflow.com/college-ojdd3/garbagedetectionapi/dataset/1
Click "Coco Jason" for downloading

unzip the folder
Copy a folder called "train", \GarbageDetectionAPI.v1i.coco\train to folder, "dataset"

like this:

Create a python file
cd dataset/train
notepad covert-jason.py
import json import os # Load your Roboflow COCO JSON file with open("_annotations.coco.json") as f: coco = json.load(f) # Build category lookup categories = {cat["id"]: cat["name"] for cat in coco["categories"]} # Build image lookup images = {img["id"]: img for img in coco["images"]} # Optional: define your image path prefix for Label Studio to locate the images IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX = "/data/local-files/?d=train/" # Collect annotations per image annotations_by_image = {} for ann in coco.get("annotations", []): img_id = ann["image_id"] bbox = ann["bbox"] # [x, y, width, height] category_id = ann["category_id"] image = images[img_id] width = image["width"] height = image["height"] # Normalize coordinates for Label Studio x = bbox[0] / width * 100 y = bbox[1] / height * 100 w = bbox[2] / width * 100 h = bbox[3] / height * 100 result = { "from_name": "label", "to_name": "image", "type": "rectanglelabels", "value": { "x": x, "y": y, "width": w, "height": h, "rectanglelabels": [categories[category_id]], }, } if img_id not in annotations_by_image: annotations_by_image[img_id] = [] annotations_by_image[img_id].append(result) # Convert to Label Studio format label_studio_data = [] for img_id, image in images.items(): entry = { "data": {"image": IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX + image["file_name"]}, "annotations": [{"result": annotations_by_image.get(img_id, [])}], } label_studio_data.append(entry) # Save to file with open("label_studio_format.json", "w") as f: json.dump(label_studio_data, f, indent=2) print("Conversion complete. Output saved to label_studio_format.json.") print("Categories:") for cat_id, cat_name in categories.items(): print(f"{cat_id}: {cat_name}")

run the python file
python covert-jason.py

copy the categories from the terminal:



change directory to YOLO folder:
C:\Users\user\Desktop\{Yolo folder}
**change the path
notepad covert-yolo.py
import os import json from PIL import Image import uuid # === Configuration === yolo_path = "C:\\Users\\user\\Desktop\\{Yolo folder}" # Change this to your folder path images_dir = os.path.join(yolo_path, "images") labels_dir = os.path.join(yolo_path, "labels") classes_file = os.path.join(yolo_path, "classes.txt") IMAGE_PATH_PREFIX = "/data/local-files/?d=train/" # === Load class names === with open(classes_file, "r") as f: classes = [line.strip() for line in f.readlines()] # === Prepare Label Studio tasks === label_studio_tasks = [] for label_file in os.listdir(labels_dir): if not label_file.endswith(".txt"): continue image_name = label_file.replace(".txt", ".jpg") # or use .png if needed image_path = os.path.join(images_dir, image_name) if not os.path.exists(image_path): print(f"Image not found: {image_name}, skipping.") continue width, height = Image.open(image_path).size annotations = [] with open(os.path.join(labels_dir, label_file), "r") as f: for line in f: parts = line.strip().split() if len(parts) < 5: print(f"Skipping malformed line in {label_file}: {line.strip()}") continue class_id, x_center, y_center, w, h = map(float, parts[:5]) annotations.append( { "id": None, "value": { "x": (x_center - w / 2) * 100, "y": (y_center - h / 2) * 100, "width": w * 100, "height": h * 100, "rotation": 0, "rectanglelabels": [classes[int(class_id)]], }, "from_name": "label", "to_name": "image", "type": "rectanglelabels", } ) task = { "data": {"image": f"/data/local-files/?d=train/images/{image_name}"}, "annotations": [{"result": annotations}], } label_studio_tasks.append(task) # === Save output JSON === output_path = os.path.join(yolo_path, "label_studio_import.json") with open(output_path, "w") as f: json.dump(label_studio_tasks, f, indent=2) print(f"\n✅ Successfully created: {output_path}")

run the python file
python covert-yolo.py

Navigate to your Documents folder
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents" && cd yolo_projects && cd datasets

create a folder, "dataset"
mkdir train


Going back the main folder:
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents" && cd yolo_projects

Activate the environment
cd venv39 && Scripts\activate

start the label-studio, (*Need to change to your folder path)
SET DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FILES=10000
set LOCAL_FILES_SERVING_ENABLED=true
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=%USERPROFILE%\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
or
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=C:\\Users\\111872\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
Label-studio start

sign-up a account


Click "Create Project"



*** get class name from the original folder, "class.txt"

click the save button.
click settings
Head to the project settings and select Cloud Storage.
Click Add Source Storage and choose Local files from the Storage Type options.
Set the Absolute local path  C:\Users\%USERPROFILE%\Documents\yolo_projects\datasets\train or
C:\Users\%USERPROFILE%\Documents\yolo_projects\datasets\solar or
click this setting!!

Select Add storage to confirm the setup.
Check Image Access: http://localhost:8080/data/local-files/?d=/train/batch_01_frame_5_jpg.rf.4b2f849f8838161c9b480a6979099fad.jpg
Go to import
find "label_studio_format.json" from "C:\Users%USERPROFILE%\Documents\yolo_projects\datasets\train"
Click "import" button

item.title: 3 Check total label in Google Colab and training
item.description:
Click Export

Choose this

Create a folder on Google Drive, "checking"

-Open Google Colab , Create new Notebook, "check labels"

add code:
from google.colab import drive drive.mount('/content/drive')

add code:
import zipfile import os zip_path = "/content/drive/My Drive/checking/your_project_name.zip" # Replace with your actual file name extract_path = "/content/drive/My Drive/checking/export-dir0912_h" # Target extraction directory with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'r') as zip_ref: zip_ref.extractall(extract_path)

add code:
import os from collections import defaultdict # Path to your YOLO labels folder labels_folder = "/content/drive/My Drive/checking/export-dir0912_h/labels" # Dictionary to store counts per class class_counts = defaultdict(int) # Loop through all .txt files in the labels folder for filename in os.listdir(labels_folder): if filename.endswith(".txt"): file_path = os.path.join(labels_folder, filename) with open(file_path, "r") as f: lines = f.readlines() for line in lines: parts = line.strip().split() if not parts: continue try: class_id = int(parts[0]) class_counts[class_id] += 1 except ValueError: continue # Print the results print("Label counts per class ID:") for class_id, count in sorted(class_counts.items()): print(f"Class {class_id}: {count}")
-add code:
import os import shutil import random # Paths source_images = '/content/drive/My Drive/checking/export-dir0912_h/images' source_labels = '/content/drive/My Drive/checking/export-dir0912_h/labels' dest = '/content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h' train_ratio = 0.8 # Get all image files images = [f for f in os.listdir(source_images) if f.endswith(('.jpg', '.png', '.JPG'))] random.shuffle(images) # Split train_size = int(len(images) * train_ratio) train_images = images[:train_size] val_images = images[train_size:] # Helper function def copy_files(file_list, split): os.makedirs(f'{dest}/images/{split}', exist_ok=True) os.makedirs(f'{dest}/labels/{split}', exist_ok=True) for img in file_list: base = os.path.splitext(img)[0] shutil.copy(f'{source_images}/{img}', f'{dest}/images/{split}/{img}') shutil.copy(f'{source_labels}/{base}.txt', f'{dest}/labels/{split}/{base}.txt') copy_files(train_images, 'train') copy_files(val_images, 'val')
-add code:
data_yaml = """ train: /content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/images/train val: /content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/images/val nc: 4 names: ['Overheating','Anomaly', 'Breakage', 'Below Normal'] """ with open("/content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/data.yaml", "w") as f: f.write(data_yaml)
-add code:
import os from collections import defaultdict # Paths to your label directories label_dirs = [ "/content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/labels/train", "/content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/labels/val" ] # Class names from your data.yaml class_names = ['Overheating','Anomaly', 'Breakage', 'Below Normal'] # Dictionary to count instances per class class_counts = defaultdict(int) # Count instances from both train and val for label_dir in label_dirs: if not os.path.exists(label_dir): print(f"⚠️ Skipping missing directory: {label_dir}") continue for filename in os.listdir(label_dir): if filename.endswith('.txt'): with open(os.path.join(label_dir, filename), 'r') as f: for line in f: parts = line.strip().split() if not parts: # skip empty lines continue try: class_id = int(parts[0]) if 0 <= class_id < len(class_names): class_counts[class_id] += 1 else: print(f"⚠️ Skipping invalid class ID {class_id} in {filename}") except ValueError: print(f"⚠️ Invalid line in {filename}: {line.strip()}") # Print results print("Class ID\tClass Name\t\tCount") print("-" * 40) for class_id in range(len(class_names)): name = class_names[class_id] count = class_counts[class_id] print(f"{class_id}\t\t{name:<20}\t{count}")
-add code:
!pip install ultralytics
-add code:
from ultralytics import YOLO import glob import shutil import os # This will automatically download yolov8n.pt if not found model = YOLO('yolov8s.pt') # Train the model model.train(data="/content/drive/My Drive/checking/train20250912_h/data.yaml", epochs=500, imgsz=640) # Validation performance metrics = model.val() # Find all weight files weight_files = glob.glob('runs/detect/*/weights/*.pt') print("Found weight files:", weight_files) # Define destination folder in Google Drive destination_folder = "/content/drive/MyDrive/Yolo_results/solar_faulty_detection20250912_h" runs_dest_folder = os.path.join(destination_folder, "runs") # Create folders if they don't exist os.makedirs(destination_folder, exist_ok=True) os.makedirs(runs_dest_folder, exist_ok=True) print("Folders are ready at:", destination_folder) # Copy each found weight file to Google Drive with renamed format for file_path in weight_files: filename = file_path.split("/")[-1] # e.g., best.pt run_name = file_path.split("/")[2] # e.g., train233 dest_path = os.path.join(destination_folder, f"{run_name}_{filename}") shutil.copy(file_path, dest_path) print(f"Copied: {file_path} → {dest_path}") # Copy entire runs folder to destination_folder/runs shutil.copytree("runs", runs_dest_folder, dirs_exist_ok=True) print(f"Copied full 'runs' folder to: {runs_dest_folder}")
item.link: YOLO_LabelStudio\3 Check total label in Google Colab and training.html
item.guid: YOLO_LabelStudio/3 Check total label in Google Colab and training.md

item.title: Combine two datasets with different Categories
item.description:
download another dataset from here, https://universe.roboflow.com/snowman1908/plastic-recyclable-detection/dataset/2
Convert both to Label Studio format first, using Convert to Label Studio from RobotFlow
put all images and jason files in the same folder.
Run this python code Merger two Datasets
import the dataset followed following steps:
Going back the main folder:
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents" && cd yolo_projects

Activate the environment
cd venv39 && Scripts\activate

start the label-studio, (*Need to change to your folder path)
SET DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FILES=10000
set LOCAL_FILES_SERVING_ENABLED=true
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=%USERPROFILE%\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
or
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=C:\\Users\\111872\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
Label-studio start

Click "Create Project"




right click and edit "label_studio_interface.xml", then copy the code.
Click "code", override and paste the code, click 'Save'

click the save button.
click settings
Head to the project settings and select Cloud Storage.
Click Add Source Storage and choose Local files from the Storage Type options.
Set the Absolute local path  C:\Users\%USERPROFILE%\Documents\yolo_projects\datasets\train.
Select Add storage to confirm the setup.
Check Image Access: http://localhost:8080/data/local-files/?d=/train/batch_01_frame_5_jpg.rf.4b2f849f8838161c9b480a6979099fad.jpg
Go to import
find "merged_label_studio.json" from "C:\Users%USERPROFILE%\Documents\yolo_projects\datasets\train"
Click "import" button
item.link: YOLO_LabelStudio\Combine two datasets with different Categories.html
item.guid: YOLO_LabelStudio/Combine two datasets with different Categories.md

item.title: Extract Geo Tag Info
item.description:
Download the Drone Sample Images https://drive.google.com/file/d/14fgSnJBAuIFlk5aBTYAKamJfeFM81uRf/view?usp=sharing
Unzip the folder
Open Google Colab
# Step 1: Install required libraries (if not already installed) !pip install piexif Pillow

add new code section:
# Step 2: Upload photo from google.colab import files uploaded = files.upload() # Step 3: Extract GPS metadata from PIL import Image import piexif def get_decimal_from_dms(dms, ref):     degrees, minutes, seconds = dms     decimal = degrees[0] / degrees[1] + minutes[0] / minutes[1] / 60 + seconds[0] / seconds[1] / 3600     if ref in ['S', 'W']:         decimal = -decimal     return decimal for filename in uploaded.keys():     img = Image.open(filename)     exif_dict = piexif.load(img.info['exif'])     gps_data = exif_dict.get('GPS')     if not gps_data:         print(f"No GPS data found in {filename}")         continue     gps_latitude = gps_data[piexif.GPSIFD.GPSLatitude]     gps_latitude_ref = gps_data[piexif.GPSIFD.GPSLatitudeRef].decode()     gps_longitude = gps_data[piexif.GPSIFD.GPSLongitude]     gps_longitude_ref = gps_data[piexif.GPSIFD.GPSLongitudeRef].decode()     lat = get_decimal_from_dms(gps_latitude, gps_latitude_ref)     lon = get_decimal_from_dms(gps_longitude, gps_longitude_ref)     print(f"Image: {filename}")     print(f"Latitude: {lat}")     print(f"Longitude: {lon}")

run and upload the images:


Compare the result:

Right click the photo, click on "Properties"
Click Details, scroll down, find Lat & Long

item.title: Google Colab and Machine Learning
item.description:
Go to the project folder:
cd "%USERPROFILE%\Documents" && cd yolo_projects

Activate the environment
cd venv39 && Scripts\activate

start the label-studio, (*Need to change to your folder path)
SET DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FILES=10000
set LOCAL_FILES_SERVING_ENABLED=true
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=%USERPROFILE%\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
or
set LABEL_STUDIO_LOCAL_FILES_DOCUMENT_ROOT=C:\\Users\\YOUR_USER_NAME\\Documents\\yolo_projects\\datasets
***Change YOUR_USER_NAME from the path
Label-studio start



Change the runtime to "T4 GPU"

Create a new folder on google drive, "mosquito_hostspot_detection"
Upload the zip file to the google drive.
Open google colab, https://colab.research.google.com/
Go to File ==> New Notebook in Drive
In the first Code section, run this:
from google.colab import drive drive.mount('/content/drive')
In the second Code section, run this:
import zipfile import os zip_path = "/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/project-3-at-2025-06-17-11-08-2f4bb7d8.zip" # Replace with your actual file name with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'r') as zip_ref:     zip_ref.extractall("/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/export-dir")

upload the images to /content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/export-dir/images
Run this to split and move your data. so 2nd Code Section, run this:
import os import shutil import random # Paths source_images = '/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/export-dir/images' source_labels = '/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/export-dir/labels' dest = '/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection' train_ratio = 0.8 # Get all image files images = [f for f in os.listdir(source_images) if f.endswith(('.jpg', '.png'))] random.shuffle(images) # Split train_size = int(len(images) * train_ratio) train_images = images[:train_size] val_images = images[train_size:] # Helper function def copy_files(file_list, split): os.makedirs(f'{dest}/images/{split}', exist_ok=True) os.makedirs(f'{dest}/labels/{split}', exist_ok=True) for img in file_list: base = os.path.splitext(img)[0] shutil.copy(f'{source_images}/{img}', f'{dest}/images/{split}/{img}') shutil.copy(f'{source_labels}/{base}.txt', f'{dest}/labels/{split}/{base}.txt') copy_files(train_images, 'train') copy_files(val_images, 'val')

Create the Dataset YAML File
data_yaml = """ train: /content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection/images/train val: /content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection/images/val nc: 3 names: ['Garbage','garbage','rubbish'] """ with open("/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection/data.yaml", "w") as f: f.write(data_yaml)
If you have more than one class, list them like:
nc: 3 names: ['Garbage','garbage','rubbish']

install unltralytics
!pip install ultralytics

Train the model
from ultralytics import YOLO import glob import shutil import os # This will automatically download yolov8n.pt if not found model = YOLO('yolov8n.pt') # Train the model model.train(data="/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection/data.yaml", epochs=50, imgsz=640) # Validation performance metrics = model.val() # Run inference results = model.predict(source="/content/drive/My Drive/mosquito_hostspot_detection/garbage_detection/images/val", save=True) # Find all weight files weight_files = glob.glob('runs/detect/*/weights/*.pt') print("Found weight files:", weight_files) # Define destination folder in Google Drive destination_folder = "/content/drive/MyDrive/Yolo_results/garbage_detection_testing/" # Ensure destination folder exists os.makedirs(destination_folder, exist_ok=True) # Copy each found weight file to Google Drive for file_path in weight_files: filename = file_path.split("/")[-1] # e.g., best.pt run_name = file_path.split("/")[2] # e.g., train233 dest_path = os.path.join(destination_folder, f"{run_name}_{filename}") shutil.copy(file_path, dest_path) print(f"Copied: {file_path} → {dest_path}")


import glob # List all training runs and see what's saved print(glob.glob('runs/detect/*/weights/*'))

from google.colab import drive drive.mount('/content/drive') !cp -r runs/detect/train* /content/drive/MyDrive/Yolo_results

from ultralytics import YOLO # Load the trained model from Google Drive model = YOLO('/content/drive/My Drive/Yolo_results/garbage_detection/weights/best.pt') # Define save path on Google Drive save_path = '/content/drive/My Drive/Yolo_results/garbage_detection/YOLO_predict/20250701' # Run prediction and save results to the specified folder results = model.predict( source='/content/drive/My Drive/Drone_images_videos/20250701', # Folder or video save=True, # Save annotated results project=save_path, # Save to this folder name='predict', # Name of the subfolder inside project (e.g., .../20250701/predict) exist_ok=True # Overwrite if folder exists ) # Optional: process each result if needed for r in results: print(r.path) # Example output info


downsize the video
!ffmpeg -i "/content/drive/My Drive/Drone_images_videos/20250701/DJI_0466.MP4" -vf "scale=640:360" "/content/drive/My Drive/Drone_images_videos/20250701/small_video.mp4"
import os import re import cv2 import folium import csv from datetime import datetime, timedelta from ultralytics import YOLO # --- Parse SRT with GPS --- def parse_srt_with_gps(srt_path): gps_data = [] with open(srt_path, 'r') as f: block = [] for line in f: if line.strip() == "": parsed = parse_block(block) if parsed: gps_data.append(parsed) block = [] else: block.append(line.strip()) if block: parsed = parse_block(block) if parsed: gps_data.append(parsed) return gps_data def parse_block(lines): if len(lines) < 3: return None time_line = lines[1] gps_text_lines = lines[2:] gps_text = " ".join(gps_text_lines) try: start_time = parse_timestamp(time_line.split(" --> ")[0]) lat_match = re.search(r"\[latitude:\s*([-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+)\]", gps_text) lon_match = re.search(r"\[longitude:\s*([-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+)\]", gps_text) if not lat_match or not lon_match: return None return { "timestamp": start_time, "lat": float(lat_match.group(1)), "lon": float(lon_match.group(1)) } except Exception as e: print(f"⚠️ Failed to parse block: {lines}\nError: {e}") return None def parse_timestamp(ts): t = datetime.strptime(ts, "%H:%M:%S,%f") return timedelta(hours=t.hour, minutes=t.minute, seconds=t.second, microseconds=t.microsecond) # --- Run YOLO object detection --- def run_yolo_detection(video_path, model_path, fps, save_dir): model = YOLO(model_path) class_names = model.names results = model.predict( source=video_path, conf=0.4, save=True, save_txt=True, stream=False, vid_stride=4, project=save_dir, name="predict", exist_ok=True ) detections = [] frame_idx = 0 for r in results: timestamp = timedelta(seconds=frame_idx / fps) for box in r.boxes.data: class_id = int(box[-1]) class_name = class_names.get(class_id, "unknown") detections.append({ "timestamp": timestamp, "class_id": class_id, "class_name": class_name }) frame_idx += 1 print(f"Processed frame {frame_idx}...") return detections # --- Match timestamp to GPS --- def find_nearest_gps(ts, gps_data): return min(gps_data, key=lambda g: abs(g['timestamp'] - ts)) # --- Save CSV --- def save_to_csv(detections, filename): with open(filename, mode='w', newline='') as file: writer = csv.DictWriter(file, fieldnames=["timestamp", "class_id", "class_name", "lat", "lon"]) writer.writeheader() for d in detections: writer.writerow(d) print(f"✅ CSV saved: {filename}") # --- Plot map --- def plot_on_map(detections, output_file): if not detections: print("⚠️ No detections to plot.") return m = folium.Map(location=[detections[0]["lat"], detections[0]["lon"]], zoom_start=17) for d in detections: folium.Marker( [d["lat"], d["lon"]], popup=f'{d["class_name"]} (ID: {d["class_id"]}) @ {d["timestamp"]}' ).add_to(m) m.save(output_file) print(f"✅ Map saved: {output_file}") # --- Main Batch Processor --- def process_videos_in_folder(video_folder, model_path, output_folder): os.makedirs(output_folder, exist_ok=True) video_files = [f for f in os.listdir(video_folder) if f.lower().endswith(".mp4")] for video_file in video_files: base_name = os.path.splitext(video_file)[0] video_path = os.path.join(video_folder, video_file) srt_path = os.path.join(video_folder, base_name + ".SRT") if not os.path.exists(srt_path): print(f"❌ Skipping {video_file}: No matching SRT file found.") continue print(f"\n📂 Processing: {video_file}") # Get FPS cap = cv2.VideoCapture(video_path) fps = cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FPS) cap.release() # 1. Parse GPS gps_data = parse_srt_with_gps(srt_path) if not gps_data: print("❌ No GPS data found.") continue print(f"✅ GPS points: {len(gps_data)}") # 2. Detect objects detections = run_yolo_detection(video_path, model_path, fps, output_folder) print(f"✅ Detections: {len(detections)}") # 3. Match with GPS for d in detections: gps = find_nearest_gps(d["timestamp"], gps_data) d["lat"] = gps["lat"] d["lon"] = gps["lon"] # 4. Save CSV csv_path = os.path.join(output_folder, f"{base_name}_detections.csv") save_to_csv(detections, csv_path) # 5. Save Map map_path = os.path.join(output_folder, f"{base_name}_map.html") plot_on_map(detections, map_path) if __name__ == "__main__": # Configuration video_folder = "/content/drive/My Drive/Drone_images_videos/20250701_videos" model_file = "/content/drive/My Drive/Yolo_results/mosquito.v4i.yolov8/train25/weights/best.pt" output_dir = "/content/drive/My Drive/Yolo_results/mosquito.v4i.yolov8/video_results" process_videos_in_folder(video_folder, model_file, output_dir)

item.title: Merge two YOLO Project Folder to One YOLO Project
item.description: import os from pathlib import Path from shutil import copy2 # ==== CONFIG ==== DATASETS = [     ("dataset1", "dataset1/classes.txt"),     ("dataset2", "dataset2/classes.txt"),     ("dataset3", "dataset3/classes.txt"), ] OUTPUT_DIR = "combined_dataset" MERGED_CLASSES_FILE = f"{OUTPUT_DIR}/classes.txt" IMAGES_DIR = f"{OUTPUT_DIR}/images" LABELS_DIR = f"{OUTPUT_DIR}/labels" # ==== Step 1: Merge classes.txt files ==== def merge_all_classes(datasets, output_class_file):     merged_classes = []     class_mappings = []     for _, class_file in datasets:         # with open(class_file, "r") as f:         with open(class_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:             class_list = [line.strip() for line in f.readlines()]         mapping = {}         for cls in class_list:             if cls not in merged_classes:                 merged_classes.append(cls)             mapping[cls] = merged_classes.index(cls)         class_mappings.append(mapping)     # with open(output_class_file, "w") as out:     with open(output_class_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:         for cls in merged_classes:             out.write(cls + "\n")     return class_mappings # ==== Step 2: Update label indices ==== def update_labels(label_dir, class_file, class_mapping, output_dir):     os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)     with open(class_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:         old_classes = [line.strip() for line in f.readlines()]     for label_file in os.listdir(label_dir):         if not label_file.endswith(".txt"):             continue         input_path = os.path.join(label_dir, label_file)         output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, label_file)         new_lines = []         with open(input_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f_in:             for line in f_in:                 parts = line.strip().split()                 if not parts:                     continue                 old_idx = int(parts[0])                 old_name = old_classes[old_idx]                 new_idx = class_mapping[old_name]                 parts[0] = str(new_idx)                 new_lines.append(" ".join(parts))         # 🧠 Make sure this write block is *inside* the with-block!         with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f_out:             f_out.write("\n".join(new_lines) + "\n") # ==== Step 3: Copy images ==== def copy_images(source_dir, target_dir):     os.makedirs(target_dir, exist_ok=True)     for img_file in os.listdir(source_dir):         if img_file.lower().endswith((".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png")):             copy2(                 os.path.join(source_dir, img_file), os.path.join(target_dir, img_file)             ) # ==== Run all steps ==== def main():     os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)     class_mappings = merge_all_classes(DATASETS, MERGED_CLASSES_FILE)     for i, (dataset_dir, class_file) in enumerate(DATASETS):         label_dir = os.path.join(dataset_dir, "labels")         image_dir = os.path.join(dataset_dir, "images")         update_labels(label_dir, class_file, class_mappings[i], LABELS_DIR)         copy_images(image_dir, IMAGES_DIR)     print(f"✅ Done! Merged dataset is in '{OUTPUT_DIR}'") if __name__ == "__main__":     main()
item.link: YOLO_LabelStudio\Merge two YOLO Project Folder to One YOLO Project.html
item.guid: YOLO_LabelStudio/Merge two YOLO Project Folder to One YOLO Project.md

item.title: Reference
item.description:
https://pyseek.com/2024/11/garbage-detection-project-in-python-using-yolo/
https://universe.roboflow.com/smart-india-hackathon-2023/garbage_best/dataset/1
https://universe.roboflow.com/college-ojdd3/garbagedetectionapi/dataset/1
https://labelstud.io/blog/tutorial-importing-local-yolo-pre-annotated-images-to-label-studio/

item.title: index
item.description:
Webots Installation
Troubleshooting
Tutorial 1 - First Simulation in Webots
Tutorial 2 - Using Wheel Encoder for Square Path Movement (sensor-based control)
Tutorial 4 - Obstacle Avoidance
Tutorial 5 - Odometry
Tutorial 6 - Wall Following
Tutorial 7 - Maze Runner
Tutorial 8 - Pit Escape
Tutorial 9 - Inverter Pendulum
Tutorial 10 -Visual Tracking
Tutorial 11- Solving maze
Tutorial 12 - Complimentary Filter for Square Path Movement (Orientation Control)
Tutorial 13 - Kalman Filter for Square Path Movement (Orientation Control)

1 Installing Label Studio
2 Image Annotation
Combine two datasets with different Categories
Google Colab and Machine Learning
